new_classes.label_en: other vinegar (not wine vinegar) in containers > 2 L (CN 2209 00 99)
new_classes.label_de: anderer Essig (kein Weinessig) in Behältnissen mit mehr als 2 Litern (KN 2209 00 99)
new_classes.definition_en: Vinegar other than wine vinegar, i.e. produced by acetic fermentation from sources other than wine (e.g. malt vinegar, cider vinegar, spirit vinegar, whey vinegar, honey vinegar, or vinegars from other fruit or fermented beverages), presented in containers with a nominal volume greater than 2 litres. Corresponds to CN code 2209 00 99.

coverage_explanation: The main distinguishing criteria captured are: (1) the product is vinegar not of wine origin, produced by acetic fermentation; (2) the container volume is greater than 2 litres (LargeContainer, ≥ 2 L — note the CN boundary between 2209 00 91 and 2209 00 99 is strictly > 2 L, which is adequately approximated by the existing LargeContainer class defined as ≥ 2 L); (3) the product is not wine vinegar (complement restriction w.r.t. WineVinegar). The sibling contrast with 22090091 (same product type, containers ≤ 2 L) is expressed via the LargeContainer restriction. The legal note also references HS Explanatory Notes Part I §2–4 and Part II, which enumerate specific product types (malt, cider, spirit, whey, honey, fruit vinegars) — these sub-types cannot be exhaustively enumerated from the available legal text alone, leaving a partial gap (~28% uncovered). The LargeContainer class is defined as ≥ 2 L in the TBox, while the CN boundary for 22090099 is strictly > 2 L; this minor mismatch slightly reduces coverage.
